sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Toll Brothers opens Naples luxury-home community for sales
feed.summary: Toll Brothers has officially opened sales at Seven Shores, a new luxury-home community in Naples, Florida, offering 409 villas and single-family homes. The properties range from the mid-$500,000s to over $1 million, with various floor plans and smart home features. The community boasts extensive amenities, including a clubhouse, a fitness center, a resort-style pool, yoga lawn, and multiple sports courts, designed for luxury low-maintenance living.

feed.title: Mahindra Group and Johnson Controls Launch Net Zero Buildings Initiative to Decarbonize Buildings in India
feed.summary: The Mahindra Group and Johnson Controls have partnered to launch a Net Zero Buildings Initiative in India, aimed at decarbonizing commercial, urban residential, and public buildings. This initiative will provide resources and training to building owners and facility managers, helping them implement sustainable practices and achieve net-zero goals. The program, which includes a toolkit and year-long roadshow, seeks to accelerate the adoption of smart and sustainable building technologies to support India's climate objectives, given that three-quarters of its anticipated 2040 buildings are yet to be constructed.

feed.title: BRID Forecast — Price Prediction for 2026. Should I Buy BRID?
feed.summary: This article provides a detailed stock price prediction for Bridgford Foods Corp (BRID) for 2026 and beyond, based on an advanced algorithmic analysis. It concludes with a "Strong Sell" recommendation due to negative technical signals, bearish moving average trends, and rising short-sell activity. The analysis projects significant volatility and outlines monthly price targets and potential returns for BRID throughout 2026.

feed.title: Does Mixed 2025 Earnings Quality Change The Bull Case For Brighthouse Financial (BHF)?
feed.summary: Brighthouse Financial reported mixed results for Q4 and full-year 2025, with annual revenue increasing but quarterly net income declining. This mixed earnings quality raises questions about the company's investment narrative, particularly concerning the sustainability of high-quality earnings amidst product and capital complexity. Despite the volatility, management's commitment to servicing capital providers through consistent preferred share dividends is noted, while analysts remain divided on the company's future growth and fair value.

feed.title: Synopsys (SNPS) Recently Broke Out Above the 20-Day Moving Average
feed.summary: Synopsys (SNPS) has recently shown a bullish technical trend, crossing above its 20-day moving average. This, combined with a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold) and positive earnings estimate revisions, suggests potential for further stock gains. Investors are advised to monitor SNPS for continued upward movement.

feed.title: Brokers Suggest Investing in Howmet (HWM): Read This Before Placing a Bet
feed.summary: This article analyzes brokerage recommendations for Howmet (HWM), noting its average brokerage recommendation (ABR) of 1.30, indicating a strong buy. However, it cautions against investing solely based on ABR due to potential analyst bias toward positive ratings. The article suggests using the Zacks Rank, a proprietary stock rating tool, to validate investment decisions, as it is driven by earnings estimate revisions and has a strong predictive track record for stock price movements.

feed.title: Is It Worth Investing in Lam Research (LRCX) Based on Wall Street's Bullish Views?
feed.summary: Lam Research (LRCX) currently holds an average brokerage recommendation (ABR) of 1.52, indicating a strong buy leaning, yet investors are cautioned against relying solely on these recommendations due to potential positive bias from brokerage firms. The article suggests using the Zacks Rank, a proprietary stock rating tool driven by earnings estimate revisions, as a more reliable indicator for investment decisions. It highlights that LRCX has a Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy) due to increased earnings estimates, making the ABR a useful guide for investors only when validated with tools like the Zacks Rank.

feed.title: Truist reiterates Buy on Camden Property Trust stock, $118 target
feed.summary: Truist Securities has reiterated a Buy rating on Camden Property Trust (NYSE:CPT) with a $118 price target, despite the stock currently trading at $106.26 and InvestingPro suggesting it may be overvalued. The firm revised its 2026 and 2027 normalized funds from operations estimates downwards slightly due to lower projected same-store revenue growth. Camden is also actively marketing its Southern California assets for sale, intending to redeploy the proceeds into acquisitions and stock repurchases.

feed.title: AMD Stock and Nvidia Are on a Tough Run. New U.S. Chip Export Rules Won’t Help.
feed.summary: New U.S. rules governing the export of artificial intelligence processors are expected to negatively impact Nvidia and Advanced Micro Devices. Both companies, which have seen a decline in their stock market performance, are counting on significant overseas chip sales, which these new regulations could hinder.

feed.title: Is Newmont (NEM) a Buy as Wall Street Analysts Look Optimistic?
feed.summary: Newmont Corporation (NEM) has a strong average brokerage recommendation (ABR) of 1.35, indicating a "Strong Buy." However, the article cautions against relying solely on these recommendations due to potential positive bias from brokerage firms. It suggests validating ABR with the Zacks Rank, a proprietary stock rating tool that uses earnings estimate revisions, noting that Newmont currently holds a Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy).

feed.title: SMCI Revenue Jumped 123% Year-Over-Year: Here’s the 85% Upside Scenario
feed.summary: Super Micro Computer (SMCI) reported a 123% year-over-year revenue increase in Q2 FY2026, reaching $12.7 billion, and raised its full-year revenue floor to $40 billion, yet its stock trades nearly 48% below its 52-week high due to margin compression. The company's strategy for margin recovery hinges on its Data Center Building Block Solutions (DCBBS) and new higher-margin product lines. Despite a cautious Wall Street, TIKR's valuation model suggests an 84.9% upside, contingent on the success of these margin improvement initiatives.

feed.title: Costco Earnings: Margin Expansion and Membership Growth Shine
feed.summary: Costco Wholesale reported strong fiscal-second-quarter results, with net sales growing 9.1% and comparable sales up 6.7%. The company saw significant membership fee income growth of 14% and expansion in its gross margins, reinforcing its wide economic moat. While Morningstar plans to raise its fair value estimate for Costco, it views the stock as overvalued given current market pricing implies unrealistic EPS growth expectations.

feed.title: Truist Raises Its Price Target on BridgeBio Pharma, Inc. (BBIO) to $95 and Maintains a Buy Rating
feed.summary: Truist has raised its price target for BridgeBio Pharma, Inc. (BBIO) to $95 from $86, maintaining a Buy rating after the company pre-announced strong Q4 Attruby sales. Morgan Stanley also increased its price target to $98. BridgeBio Pharma reported Q4 revenue of $154.18M, exceeding consensus estimates, and highlighted three successful Phase 3 readouts, demonstrating the rigor of its science in developing medicines for genetic diseases.

feed.title: Is Ameriprise Financial Stock Underperforming the Nasdaq?
feed.summary: Ameriprise Financial (AMP) stock has underperformed the Nasdaq Composite (NASX) year-to-date and over the past year, despite strong Q4 2025 earnings. The underperformance is attributed to weaker investor sentiment towards financial and wealth management stocks due to market volatility, interest rate concerns, and fears of AI disruption in advisory services. Analysts, however, maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for AMP with a 20% upside potential.

feed.title: BP seeks award of at least $3.7 billion from Venture Global arbitration win, Venture Global filing says
feed.summary: BP is demanding at least $3.7 billion in damages, and potentially over $6.0 billion, plus interest and legal fees, from its arbitration victory against Venture Global concerning liquefied natural gas (LNG) cargoes. This claim stems from Venture Global's failure to deliver LNG under a long-term contract that was supposed to begin in late 2022. Venture Global, however, has stated that BP's requested damages are "unserious and not supported by evidence or controlling law."

feed.title: Inside Digital Realty: Why US data center demand is exploding
feed.summary: The article discusses the exploding demand for data centers in the US, driven by generative AI and enterprises consolidating server rooms. It highlights Digital Realty's role in providing modern data center solutions that offer high-density power, low-latency connectivity, and compliance, serving as a critical infrastructure for hybrid-cloud strategies. The analysis covers the benefits of colocation and cloud adjacency offered by Digital Realty, along with considerations for cost and vendor lock-in.

feed.title: Conagra Brands to Expand Manufacturing Operations in Fayetteville, Arkansas
feed.summary: Conagra Brands is investing approximately $220 million to expand its manufacturing facility in Fayetteville, Arkansas, a project expected to create over 100 new jobs within the next five years. This expansion will significantly increase the company's chicken production capacity, reinforcing its commitment to the local community and supporting innovation in its protein portfolio. The move was praised by state and local officials, highlighting Fayetteville's role as a hub for food innovation and manufacturing.

feed.title: Henry Schein Opens Dallas Discovery Center
feed.summary: Henry Schein has opened its new Dallas Discovery Center, an 8,300-square-foot training facility for healthcare education, technology, and innovation. The center aims to integrate dental and medical training, fostering a more patient-centric approach to healthcare delivery. It features state-of-the-art facilities for demonstrating equipment, holding immersive learning environments, and strengthening team expertise.

feed.title: Teradyne Stock: Rerating Is Real, But Next Leg Depends On AI Test Intensity (NASDAQ:TER)
feed.summary: Teradyne has successfully shifted its business focus from cyclical mobile testing to AI infrastructure test and automation, leading to a premium valuation. The company's growth in AI compute and memory testing through platforms like UltraFLEXplus and Magnum 7 drives margin expansion. Despite already realizing a substantial re-rating, the author rates TER a buy, suggesting patient accumulation on dips due to persistent AI-driven demand.

feed.title: COO Declines Despite Q1 Earnings Beat and Raised FY26 Outlook
feed.summary: The Cooper Companies (COO) reported stronger-than-expected first-quarter fiscal 2026 earnings and reaffirmed its full-year 2026 outlook, with revenues rising 6% year over year to $1.02 billion. Despite these positive results and an increase in adjusted EPS, the company's stock declined in after-hours trading due to some segmental softness, particularly in the Asia Pacific region. Growth was primarily driven by its CooperVision segment, while the CooperSurgical segment also showed modest gains.

feed.title: T-Mobile Unveils "Spring Surge" Promos: iPhone 17e and iPad Air Bundles Signal Shift Toward Ecosystem ARPU
feed.summary: T-Mobile has launched its "Spring Surge" promotions, bundling the new iPhone 17e and M4 iPad Air with its premium "Experience Beyond" service plans. This strategy aims to shift revenue growth from subscriber counts to increased "device-attach" rates and Average Revenue Per User (ARPU) by making Apple's ecosystem more accessible through higher-tier plans that no longer include taxes and fees. T-Mobile, Apple, Verizon, and AT&T are positioned differently in this new competitive landscape, where T-Mobile's move is seen as a calculated risk to secure long-term gains in the evolving 5G market.

feed.title: Yum! Brands Inc.: Is This Fast-Food Giant Still Worth Your Money?
feed.summary: This article analyzes Yum! Brands Inc., the parent company of KFC, Taco Bell, and Pizza Hut, to assess its investment potential. It highlights Yum!'s asset-light franchise model, global presence, and dividend appeal, while also addressing concerns about shifting consumer habits, competitive pressures, and limited growth potential for those seeking high-growth stocks. The piece concludes that Yum! Brands is a stable, income-oriented play for investors seeking defensive consumer exposure and dividends.

feed.title: Lockheed Martin Names New Vice President, Investor Relations
feed.summary: Lockheed Martin has announced the appointment of Mark Kvasnak as its new vice president, Investor Relations, effective March 16. Kvasnak previously served as vice president and CFO for Lockheed Martin's Rotary and Mission Systems business area and played a key role in the financial integration of Sikorsky Aircraft Corporation. He succeeds Maria Ricciardone, who will continue as vice president, Treasurer, with additional functional responsibilities.

feed.title: Should You Avoid QCOM Stock Amid Declining Estimate Revisions?
feed.summary: Qualcomm (QCOM) is facing declining earnings estimate revisions for fiscal 2026 and 2027, driven by bearish sentiment. Challenges include the U.S.-China trade spat, softness in demand, and eroded margins due to high R&D costs and strong competition. While Qualcomm is making strides in its Snapdragon portfolio and automotive business, the current operational environment suggests it might be prudent to avoid the stock due to these headwinds and its Zacks Rank #5 (Strong Sell).

feed.title: Conagra Brands to invest $220 million in Arkansas facility expansion
feed.summary: Conagra Brands will invest approximately $220 million to expand its manufacturing facility in Fayetteville, Arkansas, creating over 100 new jobs in the next five years. This multi-year investment aims to increase chicken production capacity for leading frozen food brands such as Hungry-Man and Healthy Choice. The expansion highlights Conagra's commitment to innovation and growth in its supply chain, further solidifying its presence in Arkansas where it already employs around 2,000 people across two facilities.

feed.title: Live Nation DOJ Antitrust Trial Puts Strong Stock Performance Under Scrutiny
feed.summary: The U.S. Department of Justice has launched an antitrust trial against Live Nation Entertainment and Ticketmaster, alleging abuse of market power that could lead to a court-ordered breakup or significant business changes. This trial comes as Live Nation stock (NYSE: LYV) has seen strong performance, up 21.9% over the past year and 80% over five years, trading slightly below its analyst target but above its estimated fair value. Investors are closely watching for potential impacts on revenue, fees, and bargaining power within the live entertainment industry.

feed.title: Southwest’s Reserved Seating Change Is a Powerful Lesson in Leadership and Adaptability
feed.summary: Southwest Airlines has implemented a significant change by moving from its iconic open seating policy to reserved seating. This shift is presented as a leadership lesson, highlighting how companies must adapt and evolve even when it means altering long-standing traditions. While some customers may feel nostalgic for the old system, the change reflects a necessary adaptation to potentially outdated practices, despite the previous system's unique competitive energy and efficiency benefits.

feed.title: Conagra Brands Announces $220 Million Expansion in Arkansas
feed.summary: Conagra Brands is investing $220 million to expand its Fayetteville, Arkansas facility, creating over 100 new jobs in the next five years and significantly boosting chicken production capacity. This expansion reinforces Conagra's commitment to its frozen foods business and supports future innovation in its protein portfolio, while also strengthening Fayetteville's manufacturing base and economic vitality. Regional leaders like Clint O'Neal and Mayor Rawn emphasize the positive economic impact and the trust Conagra places in the state's business environment.

feed.title: Broadridge Declares $0.975 Quarterly Dividend Payable April 8
feed.summary: Broadridge Financial Solutions (BR) has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.975 per share, payable on April 8, 2026, to shareholders of record as of March 16, 2026. This announcement, detailed in an SEC Form 8-K, reinforces Broadridge's standing as a global technology leader in financial services.

feed.title: CFOs On the Move: Week ending March 6
feed.summary: This article details several significant CFO appointments and movements across various companies for the week ending March 6. Key changes include Delta Air Lines' CFO Dan Janki moving to COO, L3Harris Technologies appointing Ken Sharp as its new finance chief, and McKesson hiring Kenny Cheung from Sysco. The piece also covers new CFOs for Thomson Reuters, Asana, Wyndham Hotels & Resorts, Eaton, and Red Lobster, highlighting the dynamic nature of finance leadership roles in major organizations.

feed.title: Fulton Financial Receives Regulatory Approval to Acquire Blue Foundry
feed.summary: Fulton Financial Corporation and Blue Foundry Bancorp have received all necessary regulatory approvals for Fulton's planned acquisition of Blue Foundry. This all-stock transaction is expected to close around April 1, 2026, following shareholder approval from Blue Foundry. The deal will expand Fulton's impact across New Jersey.

feed.title: News: #Jimma University, #Boeing sign partnership to strengthen aerospace education Boeing and Jimma University have announced a long-term strategic partnership aimed at strengthening aviation sciences and aerospace engineering education and prep
feed.summary: Boeing and Jimma University have established a long-term strategic partnership to enhance aviation sciences and aerospace engineering education in Ethiopia and the broader African market. This collaboration will support the university's Aviation Sciences and Aerospace Engineering Academy through infrastructure improvements, curriculum development, and community engagement. The initiative seeks to develop Jimma into a leading aerospace center of excellence, with initial plans focusing on classroom modernization, specialized faculty training, and industry workshops starting in 2026.

feed.title: Broadridge Appoints Allen Weinberg as Chief Growth and Strategy Officer
feed.summary: Broadridge Financial Solutions has appointed Allen Weinberg as its new Chief Growth and Strategy Officer to drive sustainable growth, enhance profitability, and strengthen competitive differentiation. Weinberg will identify new market and product opportunities, while Germán Soto Sanchez will focus on product innovation and digital assets as Chief Product and Digital Assets Officer. This strategic move aims to accelerate Broadridge's expansion and focus on emerging technologies amid the evolving financial services landscape.

feed.title: Silicon to Systems: The 2026 Deep-Dive on Synopsys (SNPS) Following the Ansys Transformation
feed.summary: Synopsys (SNPS), following its $35 billion acquisition of Ansys, has transitioned into a "Silicon to Systems" engineering powerhouse by 2026, moving beyond pure chip design software to integrate multiphysics simulation. The company has strategically divested non-core assets to focus on high-end system engineering for the AI-driven world, demonstrating strong financial performance and market leadership despite integration challenges and a significant debt load. Analysts remain bullish as Synopsys leverages its Synopsys.ai and Physical AI innovations to address increasing design complexity in custom AI accelerators and 3D-IC technology.

feed.title: BP asks shareholders to reject call for more disclosures, trims board (BP:NYSE)
feed.summary: BP is advising shareholders to vote against a resolution that would mandate more detailed disclosures regarding the company's investment decisions. This resolution was filed last month by a group of investors who have been vocal critics of BP's approach to investment and climate-related actions. Additionally, the company has reduced the size of its board.

feed.title: Nomura Cuts Baidu (NASDAQ:BIDU) Price Target to $186.00
feed.summary: Nomura has reduced its price target for Baidu (NASDAQ:BIDU) to $186.00 from $196.00, while maintaining a "buy" rating, indicating a potential upside of 56.58%. Baidu currently holds a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts, with an average target price of $157.89. The company's shares recently traded around $118.79, and institutional investors have been actively adjusting their holdings in the stock.

feed.title: Blue Hat Interactive Entertainment Technology Announces Share Repurchase Program
feed.summary: Blue Hat Interactive Entertainment Technology (NASDAQ: BHAT) has announced a share repurchase program, authorized by its board, to buy back up to $1 million of its ordinary shares over the next six months. This initiative showcases the company's confidence in its long-term growth and will be funded by cash on hand and operational cash flow. The repurchases will be conducted on the open market or through privately negotiated transactions, adhering to federal securities laws.

feed.title: Array Technologies, Inc. (ARRY) Expands Solar Tracker Business with Record Order Book
feed.summary: Array Technologies, Inc. (NASDAQ:ARRY) recently announced strong financial results, with a record $2.2 billion order book and 35% annual volume growth, despite a decrease in gross margin. The company plans to introduce new products and strategic imperatives to innovate and elevate its international business. Following these results, Jefferies lowered its price target for ARRY, citing concerns that management's focus on backlog growth might impact margins.

feed.title: U.S. Bancorp Impact Finance raises $5.7B in tax credit capital
feed.summary: U.S. Bancorp Impact Finance successfully raised $5.7 billion in third-party capital during 2025, marking a historic achievement for the company. This capital was deployed across 109 transactions with 58 institutional investors, supporting affordable housing, renewable energy, and economic development projects. The company's robust financial health and its significant role in tax credit syndication were highlighted, alongside recent positive analyst ratings for its parent company, U.S. Bancorp.

feed.title: SHAREHOLDER ACTION REMINDER: Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP Reminds Apollo Global Management (APO) Investors of Securities Class Action Deadline on May 1, 2026
feed.summary: Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP is reminding investors of Apollo Global Management (APO) that the deadline to seek the role of lead plaintiff in a federal securities class action is May 1, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that Apollo Global's executives made false or misleading statements regarding their dealings with Jeffrey Epstein, causing significant drops in the company's stock price. Investors who suffered losses between May 10, 2021, and February 21, 2026, are encouraged to contact the law firm.

feed.title: SHAREHOLDER ACTION REMINDER: Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP Reminds Apollo Global Management (APO) Investors of Securities Class Action Deadline on May 1, 2026
feed.summary: Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP is investigating potential securities claims against Apollo Global Management, Inc. (NYSE: APO) and reminds investors that the lead plaintiff deadline for a federal securities class action is May 1, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that Apollo's executives, including Marc Rowan and Leon Black, made false or misleading statements by failing to disclose frequent communications with Jeffrey Epstein regarding the company's business, despite asserting they had never done business with him. This alleged entanglement resulted in a significant drop in Apollo's stock price following revelations in the Financial Times and CNN.

feed.title: SHAREHOLDER ACTION REMINDER: Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP Reminds Apollo Global Management (APO) Investors of Securities Class Action Deadline on May 1, 2026
feed.summary: Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP is investigating potential claims against Apollo Global Management, Inc. (NYSE: APO) and reminds investors of the May 1, 2026 deadline to seek the role of lead plaintiff in a federal securities class action. The lawsuit alleges that Apollo Global Management made false and misleading statements regarding its business and operations, particularly concerning its undisclosed ties to Jeffrey Epstein. This led to significant stock drops following media revelations about these connections.

feed.title: BCE Files 2025 Annual Report on Form 40-F with U.S. SEC
feed.summary: BCE Inc. has filed its 2025 annual report on Form 40-F with the U.S. SEC, including audited financial statements for the year ended December 31, 2025. This filing ensures transparency and compliance for U.S. investors, supporting BCE's presence in U.S. capital markets. TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates BCE stock as Neutral due to a mixed financial profile despite attractive valuation and supportive technical momentum.

feed.title: SHAREHOLDER ACTION REMINDER: Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP Reminds Apollo Global Management (APO) Investors of Securities Class Action Deadline on May 1, 2026
feed.summary: Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP has issued a reminder to investors of Apollo Global Management (APO) regarding a securities class action lawsuit. The lawsuit's deadline for lead plaintiff applications is May 1, 2026. This notice serves to inform affected shareholders about their rights and the process to potentially recover losses.

feed.title: Coterra Energy (NYSE:CTRA) Downgraded to "Hold" Rating by Siebert Williams Shank
feed.summary: Siebert Williams Shank has downgraded Coterra Energy (NYSE:CTRA) from a "buy" to a "hold" rating, though the stock still maintains a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating from analysts with an average price target of $33.95. The energy company's stock opened at $31.54, near its 52-week high, with a market capitalization of $23.95 billion. Institutional investors and hedge funds hold a significant 87.92% of the company's stock.

feed.title: AvalonBay Communities (NYSE:AVB) Price Target Lowered to $202.00 at Barclays
feed.summary: Barclays has lowered its price target for AvalonBay Communities (NYSE:AVB) from $217 to $202, while maintaining an "overweight" rating, suggesting a 12.8% upside. Other analysts like Wall Street Zen, Mizuho, RBC, and Piper Sandler have also trimmed their targets or ratings, leading to a MarketBeat consensus of "Hold" with an average price target of $199.66. Despite these adjustments, AvalonBay recently beat EPS estimates and institutional investors own a significant portion of the company's stock.

feed.title: Fastenal (NASDAQ:FAST) Given New $44.00 Price Target at Barclays
feed.summary: Barclays has raised its price target for Fastenal (NASDAQ:FAST) to $44.00 from $43.00, maintaining an "equal weight" rating, despite suggesting a potential 6% downside from its previous close. The analyst consensus for Fastenal remains a "Hold" with an average price target of $48.83. The company recently reported Q4 earnings per share of $0.26, meeting expectations, and revenue of $2.03 billion, which was up 11.1% year-over-year but slightly below consensus estimates.

feed.title: The Cooper Companies (COO) Stock: Falls Despite Strong Q1 Revenue and 20% EPS Growth
feed.summary: The Cooper Companies (COO) reported strong fiscal first-quarter results with revenue up 6.2% to $1.024 billion and non-GAAP EPS increasing 20% to $1.10. Despite raising full-year guidance and experiencing growth in its CooperVision and CooperSurgical segments, the stock fell over 2% and continued to decline in pre-market trading. The downturn occurred even as premium lens sales drove CooperVision's growth and CooperSurgical showed improved demand stabilization and strong free cash flow generation.

feed.title: Ford’s new battery business seeks to spin profits out of costly EV investments
feed.summary: Ford is establishing a new battery business to leverage its investments in electric vehicle (EV) battery production. The company aims to generate profits from these ventures by producing battery cells for both residential energy storage solutions and EVs, starting with its Marshall, Michigan plant in 2026. This strategic move is intended to recoup the significant costs associated with EV development.

feed.title: Kimberly-Clark names Francesco Tinto as chief information & GBS officer
feed.summary: Kimberly-Clark Corp. has appointed Francesco Tinto as chief information & global business services (GBS) officer, effective March 9, 2026. Tinto will report to Russ Torres, president and chief operating officer, and will lead the company's Information Technology and GBS organizations. He brings over 30 years of technology leadership experience from companies including Advantage Solutions, Walgreens Boots Alliance, and Kraft Heinz, and will contribute to Kimberly-Clark's Powering Care strategy.
